zh.Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, England: 英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Baldwin - Baldwyne of Aylesbury於1390年出生在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波利時，他的父親Thomas William Baldewyne Sir60歲，母親Isabel Blakewell60歲。他於1467年與Lady Mary Astell在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1449年在英國巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波利享年59歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波利。

zh-hans.Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, England: 英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Baldwin - Baldwyne of Aylesbury于1390年出生在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波利时，他的父亲Thomas William Baldewyne Sir60岁，母亲Isabel Blakewell60岁。他于1467年与Lady Mary Astell在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波利结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1449年在英国巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波利享年59岁，埋葬在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波利。

nameMeanings.name: Baldwin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and North German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements bald ‘bold, brave’ + wine ‘friend’, which was extremely popular among the Normans and in Flanders in the early Middle Ages. It was the personal name of the Crusader who in 1100 became the first Christian king of Jerusalem, and of four more Crusader kings of Jerusalem. It was also borne by Baldwin, Count of Flanders (1172–1205), leader of the Fourth Crusade, who became first Latin Emperor of Constantinople (1204). In North America, this surname has absorbed Dutch forms such as Boudewijn.
Irish: surname adopted in Donegal by bearers of the Gaelic surname Ó Maolagáin (see Milligan ), due to association of Gaelic maol ‘bald, hairless’ with English bald.
History: A John Baldwin from Buckinghamshire, England, arrived in the US in 1638 and settled in Milford, CT.

variants.name: Milligan
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Maolagáin ‘descendant of Maoileagán’, a personal name from a double diminutive of maol meaning ‘bald’ or ‘tonsured’.

variants.name: Baldovino
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Philippines and Colombia) and Italian: from the personal name Baldovino, from ancient Germanic Baldowin (see Baldwin ).

variants.name: Bellmann
meanings: North German:
habitational name for someone from Belle in Westphalia, Bell in the Rhineland, or Bellen near Bremen.
in Saxony, from a pet form of the ancient Germanic personal name   Baldwin  .

variants.name: Bais
meanings: Dutch (mainly North Holland): patronymic from the personal name Baije, which is a derivative of an ancient Germanic name beginning with the element bern- ‘bear’, or possibly a variant of Boije, which may be a derivative of Boidin and thus a variant of Baldwin .
Italian (Friuli Venezia Giulia and Trentino-South Tyrol): nickname derived from German weiss ‘white’.

Americanized form of Croatian and Slovenian Bajs: nickname from dialect bajs ‘bass’ (the musical instrument), figuratively ‘fatty, potbelly’. The surname in the form Bais is found in Croatia, too, where it is a less common variant.

variants.name: Baudoin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Etienne, Adrien, Alcide, Andre, Firmin, Francoise, Jean-Yves, Laurent, Minos, Sylvain.
French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements bald ‘bold, brave’ + win ‘friend’ (see Baldwin ). Compare Boudoin 1 and Bowdoin .
Altered form of French   Beaudoin  , a cognate (variant) of 1 above.

variants.name: Bald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Inge, Karl-Heinz, Konrad, Kurt, Otto.
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo, or a short form of various compound names such as Baldwin , formed with the first element bald ‘bold, strong’.

Scottish and English: from the Norman personal name Bald(e), Baud(e), occasionally Bold, a short form of the common ancient Germanic personal names Baldwin or Baldric, or possibly a survival of Old English Beald (compare Balding ); the relevant element in each of these is b(e)ald ‘brave’. As a hereditary surname it seems to have died out in England but survives in Scotland.

variants.name: Balkin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Emanuel, Herschel.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish female personal name Beyle meaning ‘beautiful’ (related to French belle).

English (of Norman origin): perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Baldekin, Baudekin, an Anglo-Norman borrowing of Middle Dutch Baudequin, a Flemish pet form of Baldwin . However, a clear link between Middle English Baudekin and post-medieval Balkin has not been established, and the modern name may have a different source, a variant of Balcom perhaps, by migration.

variants.name: Bawden
meanings: English: from a late variant of the Norman personal name   Baldwin   or a variant of   Bowden  .

variants.name: Baldon
meanings: English: habitational name from either Marsh Baldon or Toot Baldon in Oxfordshire, from the Old English personal name Bealda + dūn ‘hill’, or a variant of Baldwin . Occasionally the name may be from Baildon in Yorkshire (named with Old English bēgel ‘bend’ + dūn ‘hill’; recorded as Baldon in the 16th century), or from Ballidon in Derbyshire (named with Old English belg ‘bag, sack’ + denu ‘valley’).
English: variant of   Boldon  .
Italian (Veneto): from an augmentative of the personal name   Baldo  .

variants.name: Beaudoin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Serge, Jacques, Gilles, Laurent, Normand, Pierre, Alcide, Lucien, Raoul.

French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements bald ‘bold, brave’ + win ‘friend’ (see Baldwin ). Compare Baudoin , Baudouin , Beaudion , Boadway , Boardway , Bodeau , Bodoh , Bodway , Boudway , and Budway .

nameMeanings.name: Baldwyne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  see  Baldwin  .

variants.name: Baldwin
meanings: English and North German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements bald ‘bold, brave’ + wine ‘friend’, which was extremely popular among the Normans and in Flanders in the early Middle Ages. It was the personal name of the Crusader who in 1100 became the first Christian king of Jerusalem, and of four more Crusader kings of Jerusalem. It was also borne by Baldwin, Count of Flanders (1172–1205), leader of the Fourth Crusade, who became first Latin Emperor of Constantinople (1204). In North America, this surname has absorbed Dutch forms such as Boudewijn.
Irish: surname adopted in Donegal by bearers of the Gaelic surname Ó Maolagáin (see Milligan ), due to association of Gaelic maol ‘bald, hairless’ with English bald.
History: A John Baldwin from Buckinghamshire, England, arrived in the US in 1638 and settled in Milford, CT.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Baldwin - Baldwyne of Aylesbury was born in 1390, in Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas William Baldewyne Sir, was 60 and his mother, Isabel Blakewell, was 60. He married Lady Mary Astell in 1467, in Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1449, in Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, England, at the age of 59, and was buried in Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Baldwin - Baldwyne of Aylesbury est né en 1390, à Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas William Baldewyne Sir, avait 60 ans et sa mère, Isabel Blakewell, avait 60 ans. Il a épousé Lady Mary Astell en 1467, à Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1449, à Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterré à Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Baldwin - Baldwyne of Aylesbury 1390 in Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas William Baldewyne Sir 60 und seine Mutter Isabel Blakewell 60. Er hat 1467 in Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom Lady Mary Astell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1449 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, England und wurde in Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Baldwin - Baldwyne of Aylesbury è nato il nel 1390 nel Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Thomas William Baldewyne Sir, aveva 60 anni e il suo madre, Isabel Blakewell, aveva 60 anni. Lui sposato Lady Mary Astell nel 1467, nel Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1449, nel Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, England, all'età di 59 anni, e fu sepolto a Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.West, Yorkshire, England: イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Baldwin - Baldwyne of Aylesburyが1390年にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアエイレスブリーで生まれた時，父Thomas William Baldewyne Sirは60歳，母Isabel Blakewellは60歳であった。1467年にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアエイレスブリーでLady Mary Astellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1449年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアエイレスブリーで59歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアエイレスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Baldwin - Baldwyne of Aylesbury(이)가 1390년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 아일레스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas William Baldewyne Sir(은)는 60세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel Blakewell(은)는 60세였다. 그는 1467년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 아일레스버이에서 Lady Mary Astell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1449년 영국 벅잉함실르 아일레스버이에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 아일레스버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Baldwin - Baldwyne of Aylesbury nasceu em 1390, em Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Thomas William Baldewyne Sir, tinha 60 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Blakewell, tinha 60 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Mary Astell em 1467, em Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1449, em Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, com 59 anos, e foi sepultado em Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Baldwin - Baldwyne of Aylesbury родился в 1390 г. в местности аилесбури, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Thomas William Baldewyne Sir было 60 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Blakewell, 60. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Mary Astell в 1467 г. в местности аилесбури, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1449 г. в местности аилесбури, буккингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 59 лет и был похоронен в местности аилесбури, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Baldwin - Baldwyne of Aylesbury nació en 1390, en Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Thomas William Baldewyne Sir, tenía 60 años y su mamá, Isabel Blakewell, tenía 60 años. Se casó con Lady Mary Astell en 1467, en Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1449, en Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 59 años, y fue sepultado en Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
